title: ROE Web user requirements – Overview - Canada.ca
content:
## ROE Web user requirements – Overview
- 2. General requirements
- 3. Functional requirements
- 4. System requirements
- 5. Appendix A - ROE Online Data Entry
- 6. Appendix B - Technical Specifications
- 7. Appendix C - Import XML file format
- 8. Appendix D -  ROE payroll extract file transfer – XML Ver. 2.0
- 9. Appendix E - ROE payroll extract file transfer - XML file layout and edit – Fishing ROE Version 1.0
If you  are currently using ROE Web and require additional information on the technical  specifications of the program, you can call the Employer Contact Center (ECC)  at 1 800 367-5693 (TTY: 1-855-881-9874).
The  Record of Employment (ROE) is a three-part interleaved carbon form completed by  Canadian employers when there is an interruption in earnings or when Employment  and Social Development Canada (ESDC) requests that an ROE be issued. Changes to  the Employment Insurance legislation in 1997 resulted in an increase in the  number of ROEs that must be issued, since part time workers are now covered  from the first hour worked. Employers are faced with an increased paper burden  that requires the use of obsolete technologies or manual effort to comply.
This document outlines an Internet based  solution which uses a secure method for the entry of Records of Employment from  the Internet.
- Employer Guide — Automated Earnings Reporting System (AERS)
- Guide for the Supplemental Unemployment Benefit (SUB) program
- How to complete the Record of Employment (ROE) form
- Supplement to the guide “How to complete the Record of Employment (ROE) form” for all school boards
- The EI Premium Reduction Program – A guide for employers offering short-term disability plans to their employees
